pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to the reentrancy vulnerability in approveAndCall violating CEI pattern. SWC-107 applies because the outdated compiler version lacks built-in integer overflow protections, which is compounded by inconsistent SafeMath usage. Evidence: Reentrancy in approveAndCall function with external call after state updates (Finding 1); Solidity 0.4.24 lacks built-in overflow checks (Finding 5); Inconsistent SafeMath usage in totalSupply and approve functions (Finding 3)

findings.Description: The approveAndCall function makes an external call after state updates, violating the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern. This vulnerability appears in the smart contract code and was identified during manual analysis.
findings.Impact: Malicious contracts can re-enter and manipulate token balances or allowances, potentially leading to fund theft

findings.Description: The approve function is vulnerable to the standard ERC-20 allowance race condition where spenders can front-run allowance changes. This appears in the smart contract code and correlates with external knowledge evidence about this well-known vulnerability.
findings.Impact: Spenders can potentially spend both old and new allowance amounts, exceeding intended spending limits

findings.Description: Not all arithmetic operations use SafeMath functions. The totalSupply function performs subtraction without safeSub, and approve function assigns values without validation. This appears only in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential underflow in totalSupply calculation and inconsistent security practices, though mitigated by other safeguards

findings.Description: Contract has a payable fallback function but no withdrawal mechanism, potentially locking any sent Ether. This appears in both the static analysis results and the smart contract code.
findings.Impact: Any Ether sent to the contract becomes permanently locked and unrecoverable
findings.Location: Static analysis: locked-ether finding; Function: fallback (line ~139)

findings.Description: Contract uses Solidity 0.4.24 which lacks modern security features and built-in protections. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Missing built-in overflow checks, reentrancy protections, and other security features available in newer versions
